heading: Moving expenses
label: 217 Moving expenses
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 217 was renumbered .section 226 of this title

L. 94–4551969— substantially reenacted existing provisions and extended the coverage to self-employed persons working at the new location for 78 weeks, made it a requirement that the new principal place of work be located 50 miles from the former residence, and redefined the deduction to include costs of house-hunting trips, temporary living expenses prior to locating a new home, and expenses of selling an old home or buying a new one.Pub. L. 91–172

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Dec. 30, 1969Dec. 31, 1970Oct. 22, 1986“The amendments made by this section [enacting and amending this section and sections 1001 and 1016 of this title] shall apply to taxable years beginning after , except that—section 82 of this titleDecember 31, 1969“(1)section 217 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as amended by subsection (a)) shall not apply to any item to the extent that the taxpayer received or accrued reimbursement or other expense allowance for such item in a taxable year beginning on or before , which was not included in his gross income; andDecember 31, 1969“(2)the amendments made by this section shall not apply (at the election of the taxpayer made at such time and manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate prescribes) with respect to moving expenses paid or incurred before , in connection with the commencement of work by the taxpayer as an employee at a new principal place of work of which the taxpayer had been notified by his employer on or before .”January 1, 1971December 19, 1969

Moving Expenses of Members of the Uniformed Services, , , authorized the Secretary of the Treasury, applicable with respect to taxable years ending before , to:Pub. L. 93–490, § 288 Stat. 1466Oct. 26, 1974January 1, 1976(1) enter into an agreement with the Secretary concerned under which the Secretary concerned would not be required to withhold tax on, or to report, moving expense reimbursements made to members of the armed forces;(2) permit any taxpayer who was a member of the armed forces not to include in adjusted gross income the amount of any reimbursement in kind of moving expenses made by the Secretary concerned; and(3) permit any taxpayer who was a member of the armed forces to deduct any amount paid by him as moving expenses in connection with any move required by the Secretary concerned, in excess of any reimbursement received for such expenses, without regard to the provisions of subsec. (c) of this section, to the extent it was otherwise deductible under this section.

Moving Expenses of Members of the Uniformed Services, , , authorized the Secretary of the Treasury, applicable with respect to taxable years ending before , to:Pub. L. 93–490, § 288 Stat. 1466Oct. 26, 1974January 1, 1976(1) enter into an agreement with the Secretary concerned under which the Secretary concerned would not be required to withhold tax on, or to report, moving expense reimbursements made to members of the armed forces;(2) permit any taxpayer who was a member of the armed forces not to include in adjusted gross income the amount of any reimbursement in kind of moving expenses made by the Secretary concerned; and(3) permit any taxpayer who was a member of the armed forces to deduct any amount paid by him as moving expenses in connection with any move required by the Secretary concerned, in excess of any reimbursement received for such expenses, without regard to the provisions of subsec. (c) of this section, to the extent it was otherwise deductible under this section.

children.heading: Deduction allowed
children.label: a Deduction allowed
children.text: There shall be allowed as a deduction moving expenses paid or incurred during the taxable year in connection with the commencement of work by the taxpayer as an employee or as a self-employed individual at a new principal place of work.

children.label: A
children.text: of moving household goods and personal effects from the former residence to the new residence, and

children.label: B
children.text: of traveling (including lodging) from the former residence to the new place of residence.

children.heading: Individuals other than taxpayer
children.label: 2 Individuals other than taxpayer
children.text: In the case of any individual other than the taxpayer, expenses referred to in paragraph (1) shall be taken into account only if such individual has both the former residence and the new residence as his principal place of abode and is a member of the taxpayer’s household.

children.label: A
children.text: is at least 50 miles farther from his former residence than was his former principal place of work, or

children.label: B
children.text: if he had no former principal place of work, is at least 50 miles from his former residence, and

children.label: A
children.text: during the 12-month period immediately following his arrival in the general location of his new principal place of work, the taxpayer is a full-time employee, in such general location, during at least 39 weeks, or

children.label: B
children.text: during the 24-month period immediately following his arrival in the general location of his new principal place of work, the taxpayer is a full-time employee or performs services as a self-employed individual on a full-time basis, in such general location, during at least 78 weeks, of which not less than 39 weeks are during the 12-month period referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Rules for application of subsection (c)(2)
children.label: d Rules for application of subsection (c)(2)
A
children.text: death or disability, or

children.label: B
children.text: involuntary separation (other than for willful misconduct) from the service of, or transfer for the benefit of, an employer after obtaining full-time employment in which the taxpayer could reasonably have been expected to satisfy such condition.

If a taxpayer has not satisfied the condition of subsection (c)(2) before the time prescribed by law (including extensions thereof) for filing the return for the taxable year during which he paid or incurred moving expenses which would otherwise be deductible under this section, but may still satisfy such condition, then such expenses may (at the election of the taxpayer) be deducted for such taxable year notwithstanding subsection (c)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: for any taxable year moving expenses have been deducted in accordance with the rule provided in paragraph (2), and

children.label: B
children.text: the condition of subsection (c)(2) cannot be satisfied at the close of a subsequent taxable year,

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 103–66, title XIII, § 13213(a)(2)(A)107 Stat. 473Aug. 10, 1993
children.label: e Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 103–66, title XIII, § 13213(a)(2)(A)107 Stat. 473Aug. 10, 1993

children.text: as the owner of the entire interest in an unincorporated trade or business, or
as a partner in a partnership carrying on a trade or business.

children.heading: Rules for members of the Armed Forces of the United States
children.label: g Rules for members of the Armed Forces of the United States

children.text: the limitations under subsection (c) shall not apply;

any moving and storage expenses which are furnished in kind (or for which reimbursement or an allowance is provided, but only to the extent of the expenses paid or incurred) to such member, his spouse, or his dependents, shall not be includible in gross income, and no reporting with respect to such expenses shall be required by the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary of Transportation, as the case may be; and

children.label: A
children.text: as if his spouse commenced work as an employee at a new principal place of work at such location; and

children.label: B
children.text: without regard to the limitations under subsection (c).

children.heading: Special rules for foreign moves
children.label: h Special rules for foreign moves
A
children.text: of moving household goods and personal effects to and from storage, and

children.label: B
children.text: of storing such goods and effects for part or all of the period during which the new place of work continues to be the taxpayer’s principal place of work.

children.heading: Foreign move
children.label: 2 Foreign move
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “foreign move” means the commencement of work by the taxpayer at a new principal place of work located outside the United States.

children.heading: United States defined
children.label: 3 United States defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection and subsection (i), the term “United States” includes the possessions of the United States.

children.heading: Allowance of deductions in case of retirees or decedents who were working abroad
children.label: i Allowance of deductions in case of retirees or decedents who were working abroad
A
children.text: this section (other than subsection (h)) shall be applied with respect to such expenses as if they were incurred in connection with the commencement of work by the taxpayer as an employee at a new principal place of work located within the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: the limitations of subsection (c)(2) shall not apply.

children.label: A
children.text: which are incurred by an individual whose former principal place of work and former residence were outside the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: which are incurred for a move to a new residence in the United States in connection with the bona fide retirement of the individual.

children.label: A
children.text: which are paid or incurred by the spouse or any dependent of any decedent who (as of the time of his death) had a principal place of work outside the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: which are incurred for a move which begins within 6 months after the death of such decedent and which is to a residence in the United States from a former residence outside the United States which (as of the time of the decedent’s death) was the residence of such decedent and the individual paying or incurring the expense.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: j Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Suspension of deduction for taxable years beginning after 2017
children.label: k Suspension of deduction for taxable years beginning after 2017

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except in the case of an individual to whom subsection (g) applies, this section shall not apply to any taxable year beginning after .December 31, 2017

children.heading: Members of the intelligence community
children.label: 2 Members of the intelligence community
children.text: An employee or new appointee of the intelligence community (as defined in section 3 of the National Security Act of 1947 ()) (other than a member of the Armed Forces of the United States) who moves pursuant to a change in assignment which requires relocation shall be treated for purposes of this section in the same manner as an individual to whom subsection (g) applies.50 U.S.C. 3003
